Searching for "blocking_send" in tokio docs and notify examples...

Found relevant pattern: use `tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender::blocking_send` requires
the channel itself to be `Send`. The issue is that `mpsc::Sender` is `Send` only
when `T: Send`. However, `notify::Error` is not `Send` in some configurations.

Alternative approach: use `std::sync::mpsc` inside the watcher callback and bridge
to tokio via `tokio::sync::mpsc` from the async context, or use `async_channel`
which is `Send`.

Reference from notify examples:
  https://github.com/notify-rs/notify/blob/main/examples/async_monitor.rs
  Uses `tokio::sync::mpsc` but wraps the receiver end properly.

12  def add_derived_columns(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
13      df["gross_amount"] = df["quantity"] * df["unit_price"]
14      df["discount_amount"] = df["gross_amount"] * df["discount_pct"]
15      df["net_amount"] = df["gross_amount"] - df["discount_amount"]
16      df["is_return"] = df["quantity"] < 0
17      df["is_weekend"] = pd.to_datetime(df["txn_date"]).dt.dayofweek >= 5
18      return df
19  
20  def aggregate_by_customer(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
21      result = df.groupby("customer_id").agg(
22          total_spent=("net_amount", "sum"),
23          txn_count=("txn_id", "count"),
24          avg_discount=("discount_pct", "mean"),
25          return_count=("is_return", "sum"),
26          weekend_ratio=("is_weekend", "mean"),
27      ).reset_index()
28      return result
29  
30  def run_pipeline() -> pd.DataFrame:
31      df = load_transactions()
32      df = add_derived_columns(df)
33      result = aggregate_by_customer(df)
34      return result
